summary: Dark Star > Eyes > China Doll saga with tight second‑set flow
blurb: The show shines on a soaring Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll medley, backed by strong rhythm section grooves and vivid keyboard colors. Jerry’s lead work is spectacular, Phil lays down jazzy bass vamping, and the setlist delivers classic ’73 gems.
review: Portland ’73 captures the Dead at a creative peak, especially in the second set where Dark Star unfurls into Eyes Of The World and then blossoms into China Doll – a narrative arc that reviewers call “towering” and “epic.” Jerry Garcia’s guitar work is highlighted for its dual‑guitar interplay and a tone that many describe as "special" and "amazing," while Phil Lesh provides jazzy, free‑form vamping that pulls the improvisations forward. Keith’s organ adds tasteful color in Dark Star and shines on Mexicali Blues and China Cat Sunflower, complementing Mickey Hart’s percolating rhythms and high‑hat accents. The recording quality is uniformly excellent across the soundboard sources, with clean separation and no major dropouts; the Miller SBD even improves after a rough start to deliver crystal‑clear sound for the climactic second set. Overall, this show stands out as one of the band’s strongest performances from the early ’73 era, offering both tight songcraft and expansive jams that reward repeated listening.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, praising the Dark Star > Eyes > China Doll medley, dual‑guitar work, and overall performance; a third is mixed but still highlights strong moments without consistent negative criticism. This yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix and no reported issues; showcases the full set with superb fidelity.

key_highlights: Epic Dark Star → Eyes Of The World → China Doll medley that defines the second set.
Tight rhythm section grooves and percolating drums drive extended improvisations.
Keith’s organ adds vivid color on Mexicali Blues, China Cat Sunflower, and throughout the jam.

band_performance.Jerry: Spectacular lead work, dual‑guitar interplay and a special tone that shines on Dark Star and throughout the jams.
band_performance.Phil: Jazzy, free‑form vamping during Dark Star and solid bass foundation across the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Percolating rhythms with high‑hat accents that support the extended improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Early mix low but drumming performance remains solid throughout.
